rule: If inventory falls below 7 days, activate the approved backup supplier.
If a critical supplier outage exceeds 48 hours, escalate to Procurement and Operations leadership.
Notify Legal immediately if contractual obligations may be violated.
If no approved backup supplier is available, initiate emergency vendor onboarding.
Escalate all critical operational incidents to the Enterprise Crisis Management Team within one hour.
If production downtime is expected to exceed 24 hours, notify executive leadership.
Review all supplier contracts before switching to an alternate vendor.
Prioritize inventory allocation for high-value customer orders during supply shortages.
Initiate business continuity procedures when critical operations are disrupted.
Notify Compliance immediately if regulatory obligations are impacted.
Perform a cybersecurity assessment before integrating any emergency vendor systems.
Review insurance coverage for all incidents involving natural disasters.
Increase executive status reporting frequency during critical incidents.
Document all crisis response decisions for post-incident audit purposes.
Conduct a post-incident review and update business continuity plans after recovery.
